violations.text: There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
Employee was observed draining food into handwashing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked chicken) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Prepared salads and salad dressings were observed held at 44-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were prepared yesterday. Foods were voluntarily disposed by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Rodent carcass was observed in basement storage room. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the premises at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Fly tape was observed by handwashing sink and in prep area in kitchen. Insect electrocution devices are considered supplemental to good sanitation practices in meeting the Code requirement for controlling the presence of flies and other insects in a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
2 door refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the chest freezer in the basement and shelving in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
Plumbing for 3 bay sink was observed leaking into a pan. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023

The floors and stairs in the basement have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage room in basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
Openings were observed in basement storage room and stair pillar. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the walk-in cooler. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
